raw_text:
Director, Platform Engineering

CloudWeave Systems — Accelerating developer velocity through intelligent platform automation

Location

San Francisco, CA (Hybrid)

Employment Type

Full-time

Compensation

$280,000 - $420,000 + equity

The Opportunity

We seek an experienced Director of Platform Engineering to lead our platform infrastructure

organization. In this strategic role, you will oversee a team of 12-15 senior and staff engineers building

Kubernetes-native internal developer platforms, managing our multi-cloud infrastructure, and

architecting the next generation of our platform-as-a-service offering. You will report directly to the VP

of Engineering, own platform engineering roadmap alignment with company OKRs, mentor

engineering managers, and drive architectural decisions that serve both our internal teams and

customer deployments. This role requires deep technical expertise, proven ability to scale and lead

high-performing teams, and vision for how modern platform engineering shapes organizational

velocity.

About CloudWeave Systems

CloudWeave Systems is a Series B infrastructure software company dedicated to building next-
generation internal developer platforms (IDPs). We help enterprise engineering teams reduce

deployment friction, increase developer autonomy, and scale infrastructure management without
proportional growth in platform teams. Founded in 2021, CloudWeave serves over 150 mid-to-large

enterprises across fintech, healthcare, and SaaS sectors.

Day-to-Day

Lead, mentor, and develop a team of 12-15 platform engineers and engineering managers,

including 1:1 management of 2-3 direct manager reports and dotted-line oversight of principal-level
IC platform architects

Define and execute the 18-month platform engineering roadmap in collaboration with VP of
Engineering, Product, and Customer Success teams; establish quarterly OKRs and align team

velocity metrics

Architect strategic initiatives including Kubernetes-based multi-tenant customer environments,
Terraform-managed infrastructure-as-code practices, and Backstage-powered developer portals at

scale

Conduct architectural reviews on critical platform components, establishing best practices for Helm
charts, GitOps workflows, observability patterns, and Go/Python microservice deployments

Drive engineering excellence through hiring, leveling frameworks, compensation reviews, and
career development programs; build organizational capacity to support 3x platform growth

Partner with VP of Engineering on organizational design, resource allocation, and talent acquisition
for platform team expansion across US and future EMEA region

 Own platform reliability and operational excellence metrics (uptime SLOs, incident response times,

MTTR); establish blameless postmortems and drive infrastructure hardening

Lead cross-functional initiatives with Product and Sales teams around platform scalability

requirements, customer success metrics, and competitive positioning in the IDP market

Represent platform engineering in technical due diligence processes, board updates, and strategic

vendor evaluations (Datadog, PagerDuty, Terraform Cloud partnerships)

You Have

10+ years of software engineering experience with 5+ years in platform, infrastructure, or DevOps

engineering roles

3+ years of direct people management experience, including proven track record of growing,

mentoring, and promoting engineering teams to senior levels

Deep hands-on expertise in Kubernetes architecture, multi-cluster orchestration, and container

networking (CNI/service mesh experience preferred)

Proficiency with Infrastructure-as-Code tooling, specifically Terraform; experience managing multi-

cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP/Azure) and GitOps workflows

Demonstrated expertise building or operating large-scale internal developer platforms, CI/CD
infrastructure, or developer experience tooling

Strong systems design and architectural thinking; ability to evaluate and recommend technologies
like Backstage, Argo CD, Vault, and Prometheus at organizational scale

Experience with Go and/or Python for infrastructure automation, tooling development, or platform
services

Ability to communicate technical strategy and trade-offs to executive leadership, boards, and cross-
functional stakeholders

Bonus

Experience at a Series A-C infrastructure or platform software company, or at a large tech company
building internal platforms for thousands of developers

Hands-on contribution to open-source infrastructure projects (Kubernetes, Terraform, Backstage, or

similar ecosystems)

Background in Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) with experience establishing SLOs, observability,
and on-call practices

Familiarity with Helm, ArgoCD, Flux, or other advanced Kubernetes deployment patterns in
production multi-tenant environments

Experience with security and compliance requirements in highly regulated industries (fintech,
healthcare) at platform scale

Our Stack

Kubernetes (multi-cluster management, networking, RBAC)   Terraform & HCL (infrastructure-as-code, state management)

Backstage (developer portal, service catalog, scaﬀolding)   Go (platform services, tooling, operators)

Python (automation, infrastructure scripts, observability)   Helm (package management, templating, GitOps)

 ArgoCD (continuous deployment, GitOps orchestration)   HashiCorp Vault (secrets management, PKI)

Prometheus & Grafana (monitoring, alerting, observability)   PostgreSQL (metadata stores, state management)

What We Offer

Competitive salary + equity package (0.25%-0.50% depending on background) with quarterly

refresh cycles

Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with 90% premium coverage for

employees and dependents

401(k) matching (4% vesting over 4 years) and financial planning support via Fidelity

$8,000 annual professional development budget (conferences, training, certifications; rollover

allowed)

Flexible work arrangement (hybrid 3 days/week onsite in SF; fully remote approved for right

candidates)

Generous PTO (unlimited accrual, minimum 20 days encouraged) plus 10 company holidays and 1

week winter shutdown

Parental leave (16 weeks primary, 12 weeks secondary) and fertility/adoption support

Mental health support (Ginger coaching, BetterHelp access), wellness stipend ($1,500/year), and
on-campus gym

Home office setup stipend ($2,500) and equipment reimbursement for remote-friendly setup

Apply

Submit a resume, cover letter (1 page), and optionally a link to your GitHub or technical blog via our
careers portal at careers.cloudweave.io/director-platform-engineer. Include a brief statement on your

leadership philosophy and key infrastructure/platform decisions you've led at scale. We also
recommend including a reference from a technical peer or former direct report. Recruitment pipeline:

initial screen (30 min, recruiter) → technical deep-dive (90 min, VP Engineering + staff engineer) →

leadership & culture (60 min, CEO + head of people) → offer. We move quickly for strong candidates
and can complete full loop within 2 weeks.
